text.title: Pricing Research (Market Researcher) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Pricing Research (Market Researcher) application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Recommended price MUST cite a value anchor (annual hours saved × hourly rate, or annual revenue added). Round-number pricing without anchor is rejected.
rule.rationale: Cost-plus and 'feels right' pricing leave money on the table; value anchoring captures fair share.

rule.statement: Van Westendorp price-sensitivity survey requires ≥30 valid responses before the recommended band is published.
rule.rationale: <30 responses produce wide bands that overlap, defeating the survey purpose.

rule.statement: Each tier carries an explicit feature delta. Tiers that differ only by quota are flagged and require a usage-based justification.
Final price MUST be validated against ≥5 real paying customers at that price. Survey + competitor matrix without sales validation is a draft, not a final.
rule.rationale: Stated willingness-to-pay overestimates by 30–50%; real sales correct the bias.

rule.statement: Report names the billing model (one-time / subscription / usage / hybrid) with a rationale tied to value math and competitor norms.
rule.rationale: Mismatched billing models lose customers regardless of price level.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
